article.title: Ford Spins Up Ford Energy With a $2 Billion, 20 GWh Kentucky Battery Storage Factory Aimed at AI Data Centers
article.summary: Ford Motor Company has formally launched a wholly owned subsidiary, Ford Energy, repurposing its Glendale, Kentucky EV battery plant into a 20 GWh-per-year grid-scale storage factory whose first containerized DC Block systems will ship in late 2027.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Ford Motor Company has formally launched Ford Energy, a wholly owned subsidiary that will manufacture utility-scale battery storage systems from a repurposed electric-vehicle battery plant in Glendale, Kentucky, [according to Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/). The company plans to invest approximately $2 billion over the next two years and deploy at least 20 GWh of grid storage annually, with first customer deliveries planned for late 2027, [as reported by ESS News](https://www.ess-news.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/).

The move formalizes a plan first telegraphed in December 2025, when Ford signalled it would convert idle EV battery capacity into grid-scale production after [terminating an $11.4 billion BlueOval SK joint venture with SK On](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/). The new subsidiary is led by Lisa Drake, who was [appointed president in January 2026](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/) and previously oversaw Ford's EV industrialization work.

## What We Know

### The product

Ford Energy's flagship product is the Ford Energy DC Block, [described by ESS News](https://www.ess-news.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/) as a containerized 20-foot battery energy storage system built around 512 Ah lithium iron phosphate prismatic cells. The system ships in two configurations — the FE-250, a two-hour system, and the FE-450, a four-hour system, [according to pv magazine](https://www.pv-magazine.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/). Both deliver 5.45 MWh of rated energy capacity, operate across a 1,040 to 1,500 VDC range, weigh approximately 43.5 tonnes, and are rated for operating temperatures from -35°C to +55°C, [per Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/).

The DC Block uses liquid-cooled thermal management and an advanced battery management system, and Ford has designed it for a 20-year operating life, [pv magazine reported](https://www.pv-magazine.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/). The company said the system targets minimum installations of 5 MWh or larger.

### The factory

Ford Energy is repurposing existing, underutilized EV battery manufacturing capacity at its Glendale, Kentucky site, [ESS News reported](https://www.ess-news.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/). According to [Automotive Manufacturing Solutions](https://www.automotivemanufacturingsolutions.com/electrification/ford-launches-ford-energy-for-gridscale-bess/2662378), the facility will host the full production sequence — from cell manufacturing through containerized system assembly — with a target of at least 20 GWh of annual output by late 2027.

A second facility, the BlueOval Battery Park Michigan in Marshall, Michigan, is also part of the Ford Energy footprint, [pv magazine reported](https://www.pv-magazine.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/). [DBusiness](https://www.dbusiness.com/daily-news/ford-launches-ford-energy-to-supply-battery-systems-for-utilities-data-centers/) wrote that the Kentucky operations include "full battery cell manufacturing — including production of electrode coils — and assembly of modules and containers."

### Leadership and rationale

Drake framed Ford Energy as a long-haul play built around utility procurement realities. "For the better part of a year, we have operated quietly to build a foundation," she said in remarks reported by [DBusiness](https://www.dbusiness.com/daily-news/ford-launches-ford-energy-to-supply-battery-systems-for-utilities-data-centers/). "U.S. demand for dispatchable, bankable energy storage is accelerating," Drake added, arguing that utility customers "need suppliers who will be there in year 10 to honor a warranty claim" and that "that is the gap Ford Energy is built to fill."

In a separate statement quoted by [Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/), Drake said the company is now "executing — securing supply chains, readying our manufacturing sites and aligning our technology with the massive demand for domestic energy storage."

### Target customers

Ford Energy is aimed at utilities, data centers, and large industrial and commercial customers in the United States, [according to ESS News](https://www.ess-news.com/2026/05/12/ford-energy-announces-dc-block-utility-scale-bess-based-on-512-ah-lfp-cells/). [Electrek](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/) framed the strategic context around AI infrastructure, noting that data centers could account for 83% of behind-the-meter commercial and industrial storage deployments by 2030.

## What We Don't Know

Ford has not announced any signed customer contracts or off-take agreements for the DC Block. None of the cited launch coverage names a utility, data-center operator, or industrial buyer that has committed to volumes, and pricing for the FE-250 and FE-450 has not been disclosed publicly.

The sources also do not identify Ford's cell supplier for the 512 Ah LFP prismatic format, nor whether cell manufacturing will be fully in-house at Glendale from program start or phased in alongside imported cells. Capital deployment milestones within the $2 billion two-year commitment have not been broken out.

## Analysis

Ford Energy's 20 GWh annual deployment target is substantial in absolute terms but, as [Electrek noted](https://electrek.co/2026/05/11/ford-energy-launches-battery-storage-subsidiary-20gwh-bess/), still "less than half of Tesla's planned Megapack production" — Tesla deployed 46.7 GWh of energy storage in 2025, and its Megapack 3 line targets 50 GWh of annual production capacity from a Houston factory. That sets up a domestic competitive dynamic between an automaker repurposing stranded EV capacity and a vertically integrated incumbent that has been shipping utility-scale storage at scale for years.

The strategic shift also reframes part of Ford's earlier EV losses. The Glendale site, originally built for an EV battery program that did not match consumer demand, now becomes the centerpiece of a business targeting the fastest-growing slice of US power infrastructure spending. Whether the late-2027 delivery window arrives ahead of Tesla's continued Megapack ramp — and whether bankable warranties from a first-time grid-storage OEM clear utility procurement bars — will decide whether the $2 billion bet pays back.
